title: Bacon Brings Couples Together on Flitch Day
story: Couples who haven't argued for a whole year can win a flitch of bacon on July 19! This quirky English tradition called National Flitch Day challenges married couples to prove they've been happy together without a single fight. If they can convince a jury of their marital bliss, they get rewarded with a side of bacon. Today, the famous ceremony still happens in Dunmow, England, where couples must kneel on stones and swear they've had zero arguments. It's one of the most delicious prizes for good behavior!
activity_question: What must couples prove to win the bacon on National Flitch Day?
activity_choices: They haven't argued for a whole year
They can cook the best breakfast
They can dance while holding bacon
activity_answer: They haven't argued for a whole year
bonus_fact: The word "flitch" means a side of bacon cut from a pig.

title: Pawdon Me: Getting Out of Trouble Day
story: People mess up sometimes, and July 19 is the perfect day to make things right! National Get Out of the Doghouse Day encourages everyone to apologize for their mistakes and fix relationships. The phrase "in the doghouse" means you're in trouble with someone you care about. On this day, people write sorry notes, give flowers, bake cookies, or simply say "I'm sorry" to friends and family. Some folks post apology memes online or surprise someone with their favorite treat. It's all about swallowing your pride and making amends!
activity_question: What does the phrase "in the doghouse" mean?
activity_choices: You're building a house for dogs
follow_up_question: What's the best way you've ever apologized to someone?

title: Stick It Out for Fun Day!
story: Every July 19, people around the world celebrate National Stick Out Your Tongue Day by doing exactly what it sounds like! This silly holiday encourages everyone to be playful and let loose by sticking out their tongues. Kids love taking goofy selfies with their tongues out, while families play tongue-twisting games and see who can make the funniest faces. Some people share their silliest tongue photos on social media, and others use it as an excuse to be extra playful at work or school. It's a day to embrace your inner goofball and remember that being silly is perfectly okay!
activity_question: What do people commonly do on National Stick Out Your Tongue Day?
activity_choices: Take goofy selfies with their tongues out
activity_answer: Take goofy selfies with their tongues out
follow_up_question: What's the silliest face you can make while sticking out your tongue?
